product.name: ArWoWear T11 Flame Arc Pro
product.familyDescriptorPl: Trudnopalne z ochroną przed łukiem elektrycznym
product.categorySourceGroup: Flame Retardant, Antistatic, Vat Dyed, Workwear Fabrics Resistant up to 100 Wash
product.titleDescriptor: 99% Cotton 1% Carbon Twill, Flame Retardant Antistatic Fabric, 360 gr/m'2
product.specLine: 360 g/m², 99% Cotton 1% Carbon, VAT Dyed, Flame Retardant (100 Wash Resistant), Antistatic, 3/1S Twill Fabric
descriptions: ArWoWear T11 Flame Arc Pro is heavy weight 360 gr/m', 99% cotton 1% Carbon, vat dyed, flame retardant, antistatic workwear fabric.
It has flame retardant function with EN ISO 11612 and EN ISO 11611 certification.
Due to carbon fibers used on the back side of the fabric it has antistatic certification. (EN 1149-5)
Protection against thermal hazards of electrical arc. (EN 61482-1-2)
It is durable up to 100 washing.
99% Cotton 1% Carbon Twill, Flame Retardant Antistatic Fabric, 360 gr/m'2
360 g/m², 99% Cotton 1% Carbon, VAT Dyed, Flame Retardant (100 Wash Resistant), Antistatic, 3/1S Twill Fabric
technicalParameters: Tensile Strength | Warp>1600N; Weft>1000N | EN ISO 13934-1
Tear Strength | Warp>30N; Weft>25N | EN ISO 13937-2
Dimensional Change | -3% < Length < +3% ; -3% < Width < +3% | EN ISO 5077
Dry Cleaning Fastness | 4-5 | EN ISO 105 D01
Hot Press Fastness | 4-5 | EN ISO 105 X11
Perspiration Light Fast. | 4-5 | EN ISO 105 B07
Azo Test | No Azo Colorants. | EN ISO 14362-1
Flame and Heat Protection | Yes | EN ISO 11612

acceptedMatches.rationale: Explicit EN ISO 11611 welding protection certification directly ties the fabric to welding/metalworking protective use.

evidence: Protection against thermal hazards of electrical arc. (EN 61482-1-2)
acceptedMatches.rationale: Explicit electric-arc thermal hazard protection with EN 61482 certification directly supports use in electrical/energy sector protective clothing.

possibleButNotAccepted.reason: EN ISO 11612 flame and heat protection is present, but no explicit firefighter/turnout-gear use is stated.
No explicit military or uniformed-service application stated despite protective workwear nature.

evidence: Protection against thermal hazards of electrical arc. (EN 61482-1-2)
Product name 'Flame Arc Pro' emphasizes arc protection
primary.rationale: The product's naming and dedicated EN 61482 arc-flash certification with ATPV rating indicate the dominant intended use is protection against electrical arc hazards in the energy sector.

secondary.rationale: Explicit EN ISO 11611 welding protection certification independently supports a secondary use in metalworking/welding protective clothing.

alternativesConsidered.reasonNotSelected: EN ISO 11612 flame/heat protection is present but no explicit firefighter or turnout-gear use is stated.
No explicit military or uniformed-service application stated despite protective workwear nature.
More specific named sectors (energy, welding) are explicitly supported, making generic workwear classification less precise.

modelOutput.reviewReason: Both EN ISO 11611 (welding) and EN 61482 (energy/arc) are explicitly and strongly certified, making the choice between primary and secondary somewhat ambiguous.
